---
title: "homework_02"
author: "linchenwang"
date: "2026-03-24"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
# -------------------------------
# 1. Search for tidyverse package
# -------------------------------

# Search for "tidyverse" in CRAN
help.search("tidyverse")

# Check available packages on CRAN
# available.packages()["tidyverse", ]

# -------------------------------
# 2. Install tidyverse package
# -------------------------------

# install.packages("tidyverse")  # (run in Console, not in Rmd)

# -------------------------------
# 3. Load and inspect the package
# -------------------------------

# Load the package
library(tidyverse)

# Check package version
packageVersion("tidyverse")

# View package documentation
help(package = "tidyverse")

# View core packages inside tidyverse
tidyverse::tidyverse_packages()

# List functions in dplyr package
ls("package:dplyr")

# List datasets from all available packages
data(package = .packages(all.available = TRUE))

# List datasets in dplyr package
data(package = "dplyr")
```

```{r}
####1.Using a for loop

# Custom function: check if values exceed a threshold
check_threshold <- function(vec, threshold = 35) {
  
  result <- vector(length = length(vec))
  
  for (i in 1:length(vec)) {
    
    if (vec[i] > threshold) {
      result[i] <- TRUE
    } else {
      result[i] <- FALSE
    }
    
  }
  return(result)
}

# Test the function
x <- rnorm(n = 10, mean = 35, sd = 10)
print(x)

check_threshold(x, 35)
```


```{r}


###2.Using apply family (sapply)

#  Custom function using sapply
check_threshold_apply <- function(vec, threshold = 35) {
  
  sapply(vec, function(v) {
    v > threshold
  })
  
}

# Generate random numbers
x <- rnorm(n = 10, mean = 35, sd = 10)
print(x)

# Test the function
check_threshold_apply(x, 35)

```
